description:
Advances: specled.spec_review.diff_against_base, specled.spec_review.change_scoped_overview

FileDiff.tracked?/2 uses `git ls-files --error-unmatch`, which fails for files deleted at head. Deleted tracked files are misclassified as untracked, File.read fails, and they end up with zero diff lines — dropped from header stats, subject Code pivots, and the all-files view. Verified on atlas PR 62: GitHub 38 files +221/−2019 vs review header 24 files +135/−396; the gap is exactly 12 fully-deleted files (−912 lines) plus the 2 intentionally excluded .spec state files (−711).

Fix: partition by presence in the git diff output (or `git cat-file -e <base>:<path>`) instead of ls-files. Deleted files should render as full-deletion diffs.

Second part (UX): header diffstat should carry an info icon whose tooltip/popover explains the *intentional* residual difference from GitHub (.spec/state.json + .spec/realization_hashes.json excluded as tool-managed derived state, change_analysis.ex:214).
title: Review drops fully-deleted files from stats and file views
